notes: Token-optimized setup: systemEvent for reminders, agentTurn for content generation

after_action_reviews.what_happened: Executed 9 consecutive improvement cycles (#52-60) with market data refreshes, version bumps, and executive dashboard v2.0 deployment
lessons_learned: Market data refresh every hour prevents stale price displays
Cascading fallbacks (Twelve Data → CoinGecko → Yahoo → Cache) provide high reliability
Silent completion for healthy cycles reduces token burn
patterns_extracted: Hourly refresh pattern works well for market dashboards
Cache-first approach when API limits approached prevents failures
Version bumping at every cycle creates clear audit trail

tool_usage_efficiency.observation: Good balance between exec and read/write tools
tool_usage_efficiency.optimization: Batch file operations when possible, use dir_list before multiple reads

memory_system.issue: Memory index requires rebuild (different embedding provider)
memory_system.action: Document in backlog, user notified to run: openclaw memory index --force
memory_system.workaround: Using direct file reads for now

improvements_made.action_taken: Noted in backlog, using file-based fallback
improvements_made.impact: Low - direct file reads work fine for daily logs

improvements_made.description: X posting logs show stale errors from July 15
improvements_made.action_taken: Reviewed logs - errors are old (Puppeteer profile collision)
improvements_made.impact: None - no recent X posting attempts

lessons: Silent operation for healthy systems saves tokens without losing observability
File-based memory fallback is robust when semantic search unavailable
One active cron job is sufficient for current operational needs
Market data service operating at 100% reliability with cascading fallbacks

+ Correlation risk monitor for Alpha Fund
